note: Curated STRUCTURAL speciation for vanadium and chromium (species identity, formula, oxidation state, phase) — textbook reference facts, NOT thermodynamic measurements. No free energies or Pourbaix boundaries are asserted here; those are enrichment targets pulled live from Materials Project (get_pourbaix_entries) and PHREEQC log K. Directly supports the VANELEX thesis (V2O5 electrolyte recovered from BOF steel slag). tools/build_species_graph.py compiles this into a 3d-force-graph field/1 graph with per-node cid/ual and OriginTrail linkage.

species.name: vanadyl / oxovanadium(IV)
species.note: Blue; dominant V(IV) aqueous species at low pH.

species.name: dioxovanadium(V) cation
species.note: Yellow; V(V) at low pH; VRFB positive-side couple VO(2+)/VO2(+).

species.name: orthovanadate (and protonated forms)
species.note: V(V) at higher pH; H2VO4-/HVO4(2-)/VO4(3-) speciation is pH-dependent.

species.name: vanadium(V) oxide
species.note: Target product: V2O5 recovered from BOF steel slag, dissolved into VRFB electrolyte.

species.name: chromium(III) aqua ion
species.note: Green/violet; low toxicity, dominant benign Cr species.

species.name: chromate
species.note: Yellow; Cr(VI), toxic/carcinogenic; dominant in alkaline conditions.

species.name: dichromate
species.note: Orange; Cr(VI) condensed form, favoured at lower pH / higher concentration.

species.name: chromium(III) oxide
species.note: Chromia; refractory green oxide, common in slag.
